title: Clarke's Commentary on the Bible
author: Adam Clarke
tradition_notes: Clarke was a leading Methodist theologian and itinerant preacher under John Wesley's influence. His commentary reflects Wesleyan-Arminian soteriology and is notable for its philological depth -- Clarke was a proficient Hebrew and Greek scholar. Contrasts sharply with Gill in its anti-Calvinist sympathies.
notes: Clarke died 1832 -- text is public domain in all jurisdictions. HelloAO marks this PDM 1.0. Original publication in 8 volumes, 1810-1826. Coverage: 57 books (some OT books may be absent).
